untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options-specific Greeks (delta, vega) and volatility spread analysis that are not available in the allowed indicator set. The core logic depends on: (1) delta proximity to zero [requires delta calculation from options chain], (2) theoretical edge on volatility spread [requires volatility surface analysis and pricing model], (3) vega exposure magnitude [requires vega Greeks]. Standard price/technical indicators cannot replicate these derivatives-specific measures. This is an options strategy that cannot be translated to price-action rules.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_75_0 is a volatility-focused options spread strategy from Sheldon Natenberg's work. It requires real-time Greeks calculations and volatility thesis validation which fall outside the scope of technical indicator-based backtesting. To implement this signal, a backtest engine would need: delta tracking, vega positioning metrics, implied volatility surface analysis, and options pricing models. Recommend using a derivatives-specific backtester or adding Greeks as custom indicators.
